name: Gollizno Route and Moclín Castle Loop
name_original: Castillo de Moclín — circular por la orilla del río Velillos
description: This difficult 14.7-mile touring cycling route, Castillo de Moclín — circular by the riverbank of the Velillos River, offers historical view
region_intro: Touring cycling around Moclín offers routes through a landscape characterized by dramatic mountainous scenery, expansive olive groves, and historical sites. The region features varied terrain, including scenic climbs with smooth tarmac and views of the Sierra Nevada mountains. Moclín itself is perched on a steep slope, overlooked by its Moorish castle, providing elevated perspectives of the Granada plain. Best touring cycling routes around Moclín The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers varied terrain and views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gollizno Route and Moclín Castle Loop, a difficult 14.7 miles (23.6 km) path. This route combines challenging sections with historical views of Moclín Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir – Elvira Gate loop from Parque del Cubillas, a 34.4 miles (55.4 km) trail leading through areas near the Cubillas Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Moclín is defined by mountainous scenery, olive groves, and historical landmarks like Moclín Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging climbs. The routes in Moclín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Moclín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Moclín?
A: Moclín offers a diverse network of over 40 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate paths to more challenging climbs through mountainous scenery and olive groves.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Moclín?
A: The terrain around Moclín is quite varied. You can expect scenic climbs with 'butter-smooth tarmac' offering spectacular views of the Sierra Nevada mountains, as well as routes that traverse expansive olive groves and historical areas. While some sections are challenging, the road surfaces are generally excellent.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Moclín?
A: Yes, Moclín has a few touring cycling routes suitable for beginners. While many routes are classified as difficult, there are 3 easy routes and 10 moderate routes available, providing options for those new to touring cycling or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Moclín is well-suited for experienced touring cyclists, with 27 routes classified as difficult. These routes often feature significant climbs and varied terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045660408/embalse-de-colomera-circular-by-tozar-and-benalua-de-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while touring cycling in Moclín?
A: Moclín's touring cycling routes often pass by significant historical and natural attractions. You can explore the impressive 14th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers commanding views. The region is also rich in ancient history, with numerous cave complexes containing Neolithic paintings. Some routes may also offer glimpses of the dramatic landscapes of the Rio Velillos valley or pine-clad mountainsides.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Moclín is renowned for its dramatic mountainous scenery and superb vantage points. The ascent to Moclín itself provides breathtaking vistas of the Granada plain and the snow-capped Sierra Nevada Mountains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055470632/gollizno-route-and-moclin-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route and Moclín Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer historical views combined with panoramic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Moclín?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Moclín are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045660408/embalse-de-colomera-circular-by-tozar-and-benalua-de-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32130328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir – Elvira Gate loop from Parque del Cubillas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Moclín?
A: The touring cycling routes in Moclín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning views, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to combine challenging rides with historical exploration.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate reservoirs into their scenic paths. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32130328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir – Elvira Gate loop from Parque del Cubillas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Embalse de Cubillas&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful rides alongside these water features.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Moclín?
A: Moclín is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winter can bring colder temperatures to the mountainous areas. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Can I find routes that combine cycling with other outdoor activities?
A: While primarily focused on cycling, the Moclín area is rich in other outdoor activities. The famous Ruta del Gollizno, an 8 km circular hiking route featuring a gorge and suspension bridge, is near some cycling paths. There's also a Via Ferrata for those seeking more adventurous climbing, and the region's historical routes like the Camino de Santiago and Ruta del Califato offer cultural depth.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Moclín?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Moclín village, as well as at common starting points for routes, such as near the Cubillas Reservoir. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas, especially during peak seasons.
tips:
Visiting Moclín is usually a highly recommended experience, especially if you enjoy destinations that combine history, nature, and tranquility.

The best of Moclín:
Impressive historical heritage: The Nasrid castle and church are true gems for lovers of medieval history.
Spectacular natural surroundings: The Gollizno Route is ideal for hiking, with suspension bridges and viewpoints overlooking the valley.
Charming villages: Cobblestone streets, whitewashed houses, and photogenic corners.
Authentic experience: It's not a crowded destination, allowing you to enjoy it at a leisurely pace.

Located in Moclín, Granada, crowning a hill at approximately 1100 meters above sea level, the fortress stands atop a natural rocky outcrop, overlooking the town and the Granada plain.

Also known as Hisn al-Muqlin (“Fortress of the Two Pupils”) during the Nasrid period.

Built by the Nasrids in the mid-14th century, after the fall of fortresses such as Alcalá la Real, to reinforce the border with Castile; situated within the northwestern defensive system of the Kingdom of Granada.
It suffered severe damage during the Spanish Civil War, was used as a cemetery, and was bombed; partial restorations began in 2010 and continued between 2012 and 2013, although signs of neglect are still evident.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Castillo de Moclín — circular by the riverbank of the Velillos River route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult touring bicycle route. It features significant gradients and requires a very good physical condition, making it best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly on paved surfaces, but the elevation profile indicates demanding climbs. You'll cycle through picturesque olive groves and the charming white village of Moclín, with views of the Sierra Nevada mountains and the Granada plain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Moclín Castle, a 14th-century Nasrid fortress offering commanding views. You'll also enjoy a fantastic View of Moclín and Moclín Castle, and pass through traditional Andalusian landscapes.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, due to its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation gain, this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a high level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village of Moclín, which serves as the starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a public cycling route primarily on roads, there are generally no specific restrictions against bringing dogs. However, ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash when appropriate, and consider their endurance for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, this is a public cycling route, and you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to enjoy it.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other paths. You will encounter sections of the Camino Mozárabe, Ruta del Gollizno, Camino de las Chozuelas, and Camino Mozárabe de Granada, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the village of Moclín, where you can find local establishments to refuel or grab a coffee. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty.

faqs.question: What's the difference between this cycling route and the Ruta del Gollizno?
faqs.answer: While both are in the Moclín area and near the Velillos River, this is a distinct, more strenuous cycling route that traverses the wider river valley and leads to the historic castle. The Ruta del Gollizno is primarily a popular hiking route known for its suspension bridge and gorge, offering a different, less strenuous experience.

waypoints.name: View of Moclín and Moclín Castle
